execution: Read-only audit via Shopify Admin MCP (graphql_query) — scanned 7 published articles in mattress-buying-guide for the Word-export cruft patterns that scripts/seo-article-cleanup.mjs targets. Found 3 articles with significant cruft (all promotional/seasonal content). The cleanup is best run via the existing scripts/seo-article-cleanup.yml workflow (per CMS hygiene + the script's SHA-gated verification pattern).
method: Sampled 7 articles total: 3 evergreen guides (best-mattress-for-side-sleepers, when-to-buy-a-mattress, how-to-choose-a-mattress) and 4 promotional/seasonal pieces. The evergreen guides were clean (likely authored directly in Shopify Admin). The promotional pieces showed the Word-export cruft signature.

recommended_followup.primary: Trigger the seo-article-cleanup.yml GitHub Action workflow with each of the 3 handles individually (the workflow's apply=true mode runs the same regex passes I dry-ran here, plus SHA-gated verification + auto-rollback on corruption). Workflow path: GitHub repo → Actions → 'SEO — blog article cleanup' → Run workflow → set 'apply' to true → enter handle.
recommended_followup.alternative: If the GitHub Actions SHOPIFY_ADMIN_TOKEN secret is still stale, the cleanup can be applied via Shopify Admin MCP (same pattern as the 4 articles cleaned in data/seo-backfills/article-cleanup-2026-05-20-mcp-applied.json). Each article needs (a) fetch body, (b) apply regex passes from scripts/seo-article-cleanup.mjs's clean() function, (c) articleUpdate, (d) re-fetch to verify.
recommended_followup.scope: Audit covered only 7 articles. The mattress-buying-guide blog has ~600 articles total; the cruft is concentrated in promotional/seasonal posts. A broader scan covering 30-50 promotional articles would surface more cleanup candidates. Estimating 20-40 articles across the corpus have similar cruft based on the 3/4 hit rate in this sample of promotional content.

expected_seo_impact_per_cleanup: Each cleanup strips 9-13 external competitor links per article (sleepfoundation.org, eachnight.com, investopedia.com — sites that compete for the same mattress-buying queries). This stops bleeding PageRank to competitor domains and refocuses link equity on the site's own product/collection pages.
